top_signals.title: China’s Farm Drones: A Trojan Horse Washington Overlooks
top_signals.summary:
In 2024, Emilian Kavalski and Claris Diaz argued in “Beyond TikTok – The National Security Risks of Chinese Agricultural Drones” that the national debate over foreign social media platforms risked becoming too narrow, potentially causing Washington to overlook other foreign technologies embedded in critical systems. Two years later, we asked them to revisit their arguments.Image: MB-one via Wikimedia CommonsIn your 2024 article, you argued that TikTok’s data collection risks were overhyped and that fixating on them distracted the United States from more serious Chinese technology threats, like agricultural drones. Two years and several TikTok ban cycles later, has your view

The post China’s Farm Drones: A Trojan Horse Washington Overlooks appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: Senate Democrats urge Republicans to hold hearings on Trump family crypto ties to Abu Dhabi royalty
top_signals.summary: Top Democrats are calling for congressional hearings into President Donald Trump’s family crypto venture and Abu Dhabi royalty.

top_signals.title: Back Braddock to deliver knockout blow in Irish Derby
top_signals.summary: ACROSS the water, there’s Classic action as the Irish Derby (4.35pm) takes centre stage at the Curragh on Sunday. The field has narrowed to just 11 runners following the confirmation stage, but it still looks a deep renewal with several of the protagonists from the Epsom equivalent backing up. top_signals.title: Circus can be a Star attraction in the Plate
top_signals.summary: AFTER five days of Royal Ascot last week, you might be forgiven for needing a breather from racing, but as always in this game the next big meeting is here in a flash. This Saturday we look north for Newcastle’s headline Flat meeting of the year, which features the Northumberland Plate (3.15pm). This competitive staying [...]
